---
title: "Gasto en defensa de los países de la OTAN"
image: heatmap_otan.png
date: 2026-06-19
---

## Gráfico

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

raw <- read_excel("data/SIPRI-Milex-data-1949-2025_v1.2.xlsx", 
                  sheet = "Share of GDP",
                  skip = 5)

otan <- c("United States of America", "United Kingdom", "France", "Germany", "Italy",
          "Spain", "Poland", "Netherlands", "Belgium", "Portugal", "Norway",
          "Denmark", "Greece", "Türkiye", "Romania", "Hungary", "Czechia",
          "Slovakia", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Albania", "Montenegro",
          "North Macedonia", "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Estonia", "Luxembourg",
          "Slovenia", "Canada")

nato_long <- raw %>%
  filter(Country %in% otan) %>%
  select(Country, `2014`:`2024`) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = `2014`:`2024`, names_to = "year", values_to = "gdp_share") %>%
  mutate(gdp_share = as.numeric(gdp_share) * 100, year = as.integer(year))

ggplot(nato_long, aes(x = year, y = reorder(Country, gdp_share), fill = gdp_share)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7fcf5", high = "#005a32", na.value = "grey90", name = "% PIB") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2014:2024, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.4) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "none")
```

## Fuente

SIPRI Military Expenditure Database. Disponible en: sipri.org/databases/milex

## Nota metodológica

El indicador representa el gasto en defensa de cada país como porcentaje de su PIB. 
Un valor alto no implica necesariamente mayor capacidad militar absoluta, 
sino mayor esfuerzo relativo respecto al tamaño de la economía.
